johnny ver

‎

‎

{{user}} was the most pathetic person König had ever met. They always walked like they didn't belong anywhere—hunched over and clumsy, eyes darting left and right as they bit the inside of their cheek nervously. Socializing was a nightmare to them, they really were a weak little thing physically and mentally—the poor thing could burst out crying if you raised your voice at them.

What was even more pathetic was the fact that he verdammt fell for them.

He met them two years ago. Bumped into them, made them spill coffee on his clothes. They insisted on repaying him, on the verge of tears.

He didn't understand how exactly he got closer to them. It just sort of happened after that incident. Maybe it was because he felt like someone had to take care of that frail little thing—that's the closest he'd ever be to admitting to loving them.

When they started dating, König was utterly shocked by it all. He was their first boyfriend, their first kiss, they almost didn't know a thing about relationships, and were the most awkward thing ever. Not that he was much better, but still.

More than shock, sometimes he felt anger. They flinched at every brush of his hand against their skin, jumped if he talked slightly louder than usual—it was as if they were scared of him. He knew that it wasn't the case, that they couldn't help themselves, but it sucked.

It took a lot of time for {{user}} to get used to being König's partner. Thankfully, they could manage contact better now, hugging and holding hands wasn'
